title: Xeriscape Springy Turkish Millet Kisir
description: A resinous, springy Turkish dish featuring millet, lamination-puff, halal-friendly, in a form of kisir, served on a cedar plank.
instructions: Roll out the lamination-puff pastry on a lightly floured surface to a thickness of about 1/8 inch.
Transfer the pastry to a 9x13 inch cedar plank.
In a large bowl, mix together the pearled millet, chopped onion, chopped cucumber, crushed red pepper, salt, and garlic powder.
Dollop the millet mixture onto one half of the pastry, leaving a 1/2 inch border around the edges.
Drizzle the olive oil over the millet mixture, then sprinkle with lemon juice.
Fold the other half of the pastry over the millet mixture, pressing the edges to seal.
Bake the kisir for 25-30 minutes, or until the pastry is golden brown.
Remove the kisir from the oven and let it cool for a few minutes before slicing and serving.
Garnish with chopped fresh parsley and serve warm.
